Selinum nullivittatum is a plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae), characterized by its fern-like foliage and umbrella-like flower clusters. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of the plant may include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
